summary: Stellar soundboard recording that captures a second‑set blaze featuring Morning Dew, Playin’ Reprise and a wild New Speedway jam.
review: The GD’s performance shines especially in the second set where Morning Dew, Playin’ Reprise and an improvisational New Speedway jam are repeatedly praised. Reviewers rave about the crystal‑clear soundboard source, calling it “stellar” and “excellent,” while noting the first set is more routine but still solid.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars, highlighting outstanding musical moments and a high‑quality soundboard source; only a few note a rocky first set, keeping the overall rating just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the audio “stellar,” “great sound,” and “good sound”; no complaints of muddiness or drop‑outs, indicating a clean, well‑balanced mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as standard/rocky, but the second set is “out of this world,” with extended jams and memorable transitions that lift the concert.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His opening chord choices and tone are praised; the High Time teaser before Loose Lucy showcases his playful lead work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s slide comp behind Garcia during the New Speedway jam receives specific commendation.
band_member_comments.Keys: Post‑Brent era keyboard texture noted as retaining a hint of the old spark, with Vince’s later influence mentioned in historical context.
band_member_comments.Drums: References to “post drumz” and Mickey Hart’s guest work suggest solid rhythmic support.
